chapters.definition: A point is a tiny dot on your screen. It does not have any size, width, or length—it simply shows us an exact location.

examples.title: A Clean Point
examples.description: A single, sharp dot indicating one exact spot on the map.

examples.title: Not a Point
examples.description: A thick, messy smudge or a large circle is not a point because it covers a broad area instead of a single location.

activities.prompt: Can you mark the center of the target by placing a single point?

error_hints.count_error: You placed more or fewer than 1 point. Please place exactly one point on the target.
error_hints.property_error: Make sure your point is close to the center of the target screen.

activities.prompt: Place three points on the screen so they form the corners of a triangle. Make sure they are not in a straight line!

error_hints.count_error: We need exactly three points on the screen to form corners.
error_hints.collinear_error: Your points are in a straight line. Move them around so they form a triangle shape.

chapters.definition: A line is a straight path that goes on forever in both directions. In drawing, we put arrows on both ends to show it never stops!

examples.title: An Endless Line
examples.description: A straight path with arrows on both ends showing it goes on forever.

examples.title: Not a Line
examples.description: A wavy path or a stick with flat ends is not a line because lines must be perfectly straight and never stop.

activities.prompt: Let's build a straight road! Place two points, then draw a line that passes straight through both points and extends to the edges with arrows.

error_hints.count_error: We need exactly one line drawn through two points.
error_hints.property_error: Make sure your line goes straight through both points.

activities.prompt: Can you draw two lines next to each other that run in the same direction and will never touch (parallel lines)?

error_hints.count_error: Please draw exactly two lines on the canvas.
error_hints.parallel_error: The two lines must run in the exact same direction and have the same slope so they never intersect.

chapters.definition: A line segment is a straight path that connects two endpoints. Unlike a line, it has a start and an end, so it has a fixed length.

examples.title: A Line Segment
examples.description: A straight connection that begins at one point and ends at another.

examples.title: Not a Segment
examples.description: A path that has an arrow on one or both ends is not a segment because segments must stop at both ends.

activities.prompt: Let's connect our two islands! Draw a straight line segment from the green point to the red point to make a bridge.

error_hints.count_error: Draw exactly one segment directly between the green and red points.
error_hints.property_error: Your bridge segment missed the endpoint. Make sure it snaps to both points.

activities.prompt: Now, draw a horizontal line segment that is exactly 4 grid units long. You can use our ruler tool to measure it!

error_hints.length_error: Your segment length is incorrect. Measure it with the ruler to make it exactly 4 units.
error_hints.alignment_error: Your segment must be perfectly horizontal (flat).

chapters.definition: A ray starts at one fixed point and goes on forever in one direction. Think of a beam of light shooting from a laser pointer!

examples.title: A Light Ray
examples.description: A path starting at a dot and ending with an arrow in the direction it travels.

examples.title: Not a Ray
examples.description: A segment with no arrows, or a line with arrows on both ends, is not a ray.

activities.prompt: Aim your flashlight! Draw a ray that starts at the green point and goes straight through the orange point.

error_hints.direction_error: Make sure the ray starts at the green point and points directly through the orange point.

activities.prompt: Draw two rays starting from the exact same central point but going in opposite directions to form a flat line.

error_hints.count_error: We need exactly two rays on the screen.
error_hints.start_error: Both rays must start from the exact same point in the middle.
error_hints.direction_error: The rays must point in opposite directions to form a straight line.

chapters.definition: An angle is the space between two rays or line segments that meet at a shared point (called the vertex). It measures how much you turn!

examples.title: An Angle
examples.description: Two segments joining at a single corner, forming a clean angle.

examples.title: Not an Angle
examples.description: Two lines that cross each other or do not touch do not form a single angle vertex.

activities.prompt: Draw a right angle, which looks like the perfect corner of a square or a capital letter L. It must measure exactly 90 degrees.

error_hints.count_error: An angle needs exactly two segments meeting at a vertex point.
error_hints.angle_error: Your angle is not 90 degrees. Adjust the segments to make a perfect corner.

activities.prompt: Now draw an acute angle, which is sharp, narrow, and smaller than a right angle (less than 90 degrees).

error_hints.count_error: Please draw two connected segments to form an angle.
error_hints.angle_error: This angle is too wide! An acute angle must be sharp and less than 90 degrees.

chapters.definition: A polygon is a flat, closed shape with straight sides. It must not have any curved lines, and there cannot be any open gaps!

examples.title: A Polygon
examples.description: A closed, flat shape made of straight segments with no curves.

examples.title: Not a Polygon
examples.description: An open shape, a shape with a curved side (like a circle), or crossing lines.

activities.prompt: Use our segments tool to build a closed polygon with exactly 4 straight sides. Make sure there are no gaps!

error_hints.closure_error: The shape is open. Connect the last point to the first point to seal it.
error_hints.side_count_error: We need a shape with exactly 4 straight sides.

activities.prompt: Can you build a house roof shape? Draw a closed polygon with exactly 5 straight sides (called a pentagon).

error_hints.closure_error: Connect all the points in a loop so no space escapes.
error_hints.side_count_error: A pentagon must have exactly 5 sides. Count your segments!

chapters.definition: A triangle is a polygon with exactly three straight sides and three corners. It is the strongest shape used to construct bridges!

examples.title: A Triangle
examples.description: A closed polygon with exactly three connected sides.

examples.title: Not a Triangle
examples.description: An open three-segment shape, or a shape with four corners.

activities.prompt: Draw an isosceles triangle. This is a special triangle where at least two of the sides are the exact same length.

error_hints.closure_error: Make sure all 3 points are connected to form a closed triangle loop.
error_hints.side_count_error: A triangle must have exactly 3 sides.
error_hints.property_error: Two of the sides must be equal. Use the ruler tool to match their lengths.

activities.prompt: Draw a right-angled triangle. This is a triangle where one of the corners is a perfect 90-degree angle.

error_hints.closure_error: Draw 3 connected segments to close the triangle.
error_hints.side_count_error: A triangle must have exactly 3 sides.
error_hints.property_error: Your triangle needs exactly one 90-degree corner. Use the angle tool to check.

chapters.definition: A quadrilateral is any flat shape with exactly four straight sides. Squares, rectangles, and diamonds are all part of this shape family!

examples.title: A Quadrilateral
examples.description: Any flat shape enclosed by four straight sides.

examples.title: Not a Quadrilateral
examples.description: A 3-sided shape, a shape with curved sides, or an open 4-sided path.

activities.prompt: Draw a rectangle! Its opposite sides must be equal in length, and all four corners must be perfect 90-degree right angles.

error_hints.closure_error: Make sure all 4 sides are fully connected in a loop.
error_hints.side_count_error: A rectangle must have exactly 4 sides.
error_hints.property_error: All 4 corners must be 90-degree right angles and opposite sides must be equal.

activities.prompt: Draw a perfect square! All four sides must be the exact same length, and all four corners must be 90 degrees.

error_hints.closure_error: Connect the sides to make a closed square.
error_hints.side_count_error: A square must have exactly 4 sides.
error_hints.property_error: All 4 sides of a square must be the exact same length, and all corners must be 90 degrees.
